text.title: Prompt Portability Across Providers Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules + 2 terminal conclusions every portability-spec must enforce.

rule.statement: The methodology fires on a named trigger (model deprecation, provider outage drill, new-feature launch). Vague triggers rejected.
rule.rationale: Without explicit trigger, the migration is reactive and breaks users.

rule.statement: Output shape is the portability-spec.json schema. Free-form output rejected.
rule.rationale: Shaped output is consumable by the migration job.

rule.statement: Every provider-specific decision cites the provider's docs URL + version. Hand-wave rationale rejected.
rule.rationale: Provider APIs change quarterly; citations prevent stale assumptions.

rule.statement: Spec names a single accountable owner.
Spec reviewed every quarter or on provider API version bump.
rule.rationale: Provider APIs drift; stale spec invalidates the abstraction.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold AND provider matrix has ≥2 entries → run the 5-step procedure.
Single-provider product OR no redundancy plans → skip; use provider-native prompting.
